policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-011, CS-RULE-014, FIN-RULE-004, FIN-RULE-006, FIN-RULE-010, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-020, CS-RULE-021]
expected_outcome: Customer calls intending to cancel due to price. Hannah validates the underlying concern (cost), asks open questions to understand usage patterns, and offers a downgrade-plus-loyalty-credit combination that meets the customer's monthly budget. Customer retains. Within agent cap. PASS — full SOP-05 retention save. Survey offered (CS-RULE-021 cancellation exception does NOT apply because the customer is staying).

coverage.element: Retention playbook — discovery questions before offer
Downgrade + loyalty credit offer within cap

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Hannah. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And this call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hi Hannah. This is Olivia Marchetti. Honestly, I'm calling to cancel my internet. The bill keeps creeping up and I just can't justify it anymore.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Hi Ms. Marchetti, thank you for being upfront with me about that. I hear you, and bills creeping up is a real frustration. Let me see what we can do.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you're calling about a cancellation due to cost, and I can certainly help you with that. Before I make any changes, I'll need to verify a few details — could you start with the account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Eight-eight-one-zero, five-five-six-six, two-nine.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thanks. And the last four digits of the payment method on file?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Four-four-one-seven.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And the full name on the account?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Olivia Renee Marchetti.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That matches. You're fully verified, Ms. Marchetti.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay, thanks.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I can see you've been with us for almost four years, which is wonderful — and I can see your current plan is Home Pro Plus, the five hundred megabit plan, at seventy-nine dollars a month. Is that the bill that's creeping up?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes. When I signed up it was sixty-something with the intro promo. Now it's seventy-nine plus the equipment fee plus tax. I'm paying close to ninety a month and my budget is tight.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's completely fair, and I'm sorry the intro pricing rolled off without something to soften the transition. Before I process anything either way, can I ask a few quick questions about how you actually use the internet? I don't want to push you to stay if it doesn't work for you, but I'd rather understand the picture before we decide.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Sure. Go ahead.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Roughly how many people use the internet in your household on a typical day?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: It's just me. My son moved out last year.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Got it. And what does a typical online day look like for you — mostly streaming and browsing, or work calls, or something heavier?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Mostly Netflix and some YouTube in the evenings. I work in person, so I don't do video calls from home. I might check email and shop online but that's it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's really helpful. Here's what I'm seeing — the five hundred plan was a great fit when there were two of you and one was working from home. With one person, mostly Netflix, you are paying for a lot of headroom you aren't using.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Mm-hm.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: So before we cancel, I'd like to propose two changes together. First, drop the plan to Home Essentials — that's our hundred megabit tier, which is more than enough for streaming, browsing, and shopping. The list price on that is fifty-nine dollars.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay…

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Second — for loyalty, I can apply a fifteen-dollar-a-month credit for the next twelve months. That's a recurring credit, not a one-time, and it brings the plan effectively to forty-four dollars before the equipment fee.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Forty-four? Really?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Forty-four for the service, plus the eight dollar equipment fee, plus tax. So you'd be looking at roughly fifty-five to fifty-eight dollars total each month for the next twelve months, instead of close to ninety.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That's a real difference. I was paying about that when I first signed up.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And honestly, this is closer to what your actual usage justifies. We were never going to deliver more value with the higher-tier plan if you weren't using it.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Is there a catch with the credit? Does it lock me in?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: No contract extension required. Your current term doesn't change. The loyalty credit is month-to-month — if you ever decide to leave during the twelve months, no early-termination penalty is added by accepting this credit.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay. That feels honest.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Take a moment if you need to. No pressure — if forty-four plus equipment still doesn't fit the budget, we cancel today and there are no hard feelings.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, that actually works. Let's do it. Downgrade and the credit.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Wonderful. Before I apply the credit — I want to confirm I'm not stacking it against another courtesy credit in the same six-month window. Looking at your account… no courtesy credits in the last hundred and eighty days, so this applies cleanly within policy.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Applying the plan change from Home Pro Plus to Home Essentials, effective immediately. And applying the fifteen-dollar-a-month loyalty credit for twelve months, starting with the current cycle.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Done. The plan change kicks in within fifteen minutes — you may want to power-cycle the router once. The loyalty credit will start showing on this cycle, pro-rated for the days left.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: And the credit shows where exactly?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: The balance on your online account will update within five days but will officially appear on your next PDF bill. You'll see two line items — Home Essentials at fifty-nine and a loyalty credit line showing negative fifteen — so it's transparent month by month.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good. Thank you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You're very welcome. And I want to say — you came in ready to cancel, and you stayed because the numbers worked. That's the right reason.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yeah. I appreciate that you actually listened to what I was using it for.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's the part most people don't expect from a retention call — the questions about how you actually live with the service. But it's the only honest way to get to a real number.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: you called considering cancellation due to cost, we downgraded your plan from Home Pro Plus to Home Essentials at fifty-nine dollars, applied a fifteen-dollar-a-month loyalty credit for the next twelve months, and your total monthly bill should drop to roughly fifty-five to fifty-eight dollars including equipment and tax. The plan change is live, the credit posts on your next bill. Does that all sound right?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes. That's exactly what I wanted.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today, Ms. Marchetti?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, that's it. Thank you, Hannah.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: My pleasure.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your case reference number is N-X-L dash one-eight-six-six-zero-three-four. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up — if you have a moment, we'd really appreciate the feedback. Thank you for staying with NexaLink, Ms. Marchetti. Have a great rest of your day.
